args.report:
[Analytics Dojo SMC Private Letterhead]

[Date]

Sara Khan  
[Address Line 1]  
[Address Line 2]  
[City], [Postal Code]  
Pakistan

Subject: Offer of Employment – Data Analyst

Dear Sara Khan,

Analytics Dojo SMC Private (the “Company”) is pleased to offer you employment in the position of Data Analyst (the “Position”), on the terms and conditions set out in this letter (the “Offer Letter”). This Offer Letter is subject to review and approval by qualified legal counsel and to your acceptance of the terms below.

## 1. Position and Reporting

1.1 **Position.** You will be employed as a **Data Analyst** on a full‑time basis. You agree to diligently perform all duties customarily associated with this role and any additional duties reasonably assigned to you by the Company, consistent with your Position.

1.2 **Reporting Line.** You will report to **[BLANK – Reporting Manager / Title]**, or such other person as the Company may designate from time to time.

1.3 **Location.** Your primary place of work will be the Company’s offices in **[BLANK – City], Pakistan**, or such other location (including remote or hybrid arrangements) as the Company may reasonably determine, subject to applicable law.

1.4 **Exclusivity and Best Efforts.** During your employment, you will devote your full working time, attention, and best efforts to the business of the Company and will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, engage in any other employment, consultancy, or business activity (whether full‑time or part‑time) that conflicts with your duties to the Company, subject to applicable Pakistani law.

## 2. Start Date and Duration

2.1 **Commencement.** Your employment with the Company is expected to commence on **[BLANK – Start Date]** (the “Start Date”), or such other date as may be mutually agreed in writing.

2.2 **Nature of Employment.** Your employment is on a continuous basis and is not for a fixed term. Nothing in this Offer Letter shall be construed as guaranteeing employment for any specific duration. Termination of employment will be in accordance with Section 10 of this Offer Letter and applicable Pakistani labour law.

## 3. Working Hours and Leave

3.1 **Working Hours.** Your normal working hours will be **[BLANK – Standard Working Hours, e.g., 9:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m., Monday to Friday]**, with such additional hours as may reasonably be required for the proper performance of your duties, subject to applicable law regarding maximum working hours and overtime.

3.2 **Weekly Rest.** You will be entitled to at least one weekly rest day in accordance with applicable Pakistani labour law.

3.3 **Annual Leave.** You will be entitled to paid annual leave in accordance with the Company’s leave policy as in force from time to time and subject to applicable Pakistani labour law. The current entitlement is **[BLANK – Number]** working days of paid annual leave per year of service, accrued pro rata.

3.4 **Other Leave.** You will be entitled to public holidays and other statutory leave (including sick leave, maternity/paternity leave, and any other leave mandated by applicable Pakistani labour law) in accordance with law and the Company’s policies.

## 4. Compensation and Benefits

4.1 **Base Salary.** You will be paid a gross base salary of **PKR [BLANK – Base Salary Amount] per [BLANK – Month/Year]**, payable in arrears in accordance with the Company’s standard payroll practices and subject to applicable tax and statutory deductions.

4.2 **Variable Compensation.** You may be eligible to participate in a discretionary bonus, incentive, or performance‑based compensation program as may be established by the Company from time to time. Any such payments are not guaranteed and will be subject to the terms of the applicable program and the Company’s sole discretion.

4.3 **Benefits.** You will be eligible to participate in the employee benefit plans and programs that the Company may establish and make available to similarly situated employees in Pakistan, subject to the terms and conditions of such plans and applicable law. These may include, where applicable, health insurance, provident fund, gratuity, and other benefits as determined by the Company.

4.4 **Statutory Contributions and Deductions.** The Company will make all mandatory employer contributions and withhold all required employee contributions and deductions (including income tax and social security contributions, if applicable) in accordance with Pakistani law.

## 5. Probation and Confirmation

5.1 **Probationary Period.** Your employment will be subject to a probationary period of **[BLANK – Probation Duration, e.g., three (3) months]** commencing on the Start Date, or such other period as may be specified by applicable law.

5.2 **Confirmation.** Upon successful completion of the probationary period, your employment may be confirmed in writing. The Company may, in its discretion and subject to applicable law, extend the probationary period or terminate your employment during probation in accordance with Section 10 and applicable Pakistani labour law.

## 6. Duties, Policies, and Code of Conduct

6.1 **Duties.** You agree to perform your duties faithfully, honestly, and to the best of your abilities, and to comply with all lawful and reasonable instructions of the Company.

6.2 **Policies and Procedures.** You agree to comply with all Company policies, procedures, rules, and regulations as may be implemented or amended from time to time, including but not limited to policies on confidentiality, data protection, information security, anti‑harassment, anti‑discrimination, workplace conduct, and use of Company property and systems.

6.3 **Conflict of Interest.** You must promptly disclose to the Company any actual or potential conflict of interest that may arise in connection with your employment.

## 7. Confidentiality and Intellectual Property

7.1 **Confidential Information.** You acknowledge that, in the course of your employment, you will have access to and be entrusted with confidential and proprietary information relating to the Company, its affiliates, clients, partners, and suppliers, including but not limited to technical data, business plans, financial information, trade secrets, algorithms, models, datasets, and other non‑public information (“Confidential Information”).

You agree that, both during your employment and after its termination, you will keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential and will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, disclose, use, copy, or permit the use or disclosure of any Confidential Information except as required in the proper performance of your duties or as required by law.

7.2 **Return of Property.** Upon termination of your employment for any reason, or at any time upon the Company’s request, you will promptly return to the Company all property, documents, records, data, equipment, and other materials (including electronic copies) belonging to the Company or containing Confidential Information.

7.3 **Intellectual Property.** You agree that all inventions, discoveries, works of authorship, software, code, models, analyses, reports, documentation, designs, processes, improvements, and other intellectual property (collectively, “IP”) that you conceive, create, develop, or reduce to practice, alone or with others, in the course of your employment with the Company and/or using the Company’s time, resources, or Confidential Information, shall be the sole and exclusive property of the Company to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law.

You hereby assign (and agree to assign) to the Company all rights, title, and interest in and to such IP, and agree to execute any documents and do all acts reasonably required by the Company to perfect, protect, or enforce the Company’s rights in such IP, even after termination of your employment, without additional compensation (except as required by law).

7.4 **Separate IP/Confidentiality Agreement.** As a condition of your employment, you may be required to sign a separate confidentiality and intellectual property assignment agreement. In the event of any inconsistency between this Offer Letter and such agreement, the terms of the separate agreement will prevail with respect to confidentiality and IP matters.

## 8. Data Protection and Privacy

8.1 **Employee Data.** The Company will collect, process, store, and use your personal data for legitimate business purposes, including administration of your employment, payroll, benefits, performance management, compliance with legal obligations, and operation of its business.

8.2 **Consent and Compliance.** You consent to the Company processing your personal data in accordance with applicable data protection and privacy laws in Pakistan and the Company’s data protection policies. You agree to comply with all such policies and to handle personal data of others only as permitted by your role and applicable law.

## 9. Outside Work, Non‑Solicitation, and Non‑Competition

9.1 **Outside Work.** You will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, undertake any other employment, consultancy, or business activity that conflicts with your duties to the Company or that may adversely affect the Company’s legitimate business interests, subject to applicable Pakistani law.

9.2 **Non‑Solicitation.** During your employment and for a period of **[BLANK – Non‑Solicitation Period, e.g., twelve (12) months]** following the termination of your employment (or such shorter period as may be enforceable under applicable law), you will not, directly or indirectly, solicit or attempt to solicit (a) any employee or contractor of the Company to terminate or reduce their relationship with the Company, or (b) any client or customer of the Company with whom you had material dealings during the last [BLANK – Look‑Back Period, e.g., twelve (12) months] of your employment, for the purpose of providing competing products or services.

9.3 **Non‑Competition.** To the extent permitted by applicable Pakistani law, you agree that, during your employment, you will not engage in any business or activity that competes with the Company’s business. Any post‑termination non‑competition obligations, if applicable, will be set out in a separate agreement or policy and will be subject to applicable law.

## 10. Termination of Employment

10.1 **Termination by Either Party.** After completion of any applicable probationary period, either you or the Company may terminate your employment by giving the other party not less than **[BLANK – Notice Period, e.g., thirty (30) days]** prior written notice or payment in lieu of notice, in accordance with applicable Pakistani labour law.

10.2 **Termination During Probation.** During the probationary period, either you or the Company may terminate your employment by giving such notice (if any) as may be required by applicable Pakistani labour law or by the Company’s policies, or payment in lieu of such notice where permitted by law.

10.3 **Termination for Cause.** The Company may terminate your employment without notice or payment in lieu of notice for cause, including but not limited to serious misconduct, willful disobedience, gross negligence, fraud, dishonesty, breach of confidentiality, or any other ground recognized under applicable Pakistani labour law.

10.4 **Final Settlement.** Upon termination of your employment for any reason, the Company will pay you all salary and benefits due up to the last working day, together with any accrued but unused statutory leave and any other amounts required by applicable law, subject to lawful deductions.

10.5 **Relieving and Experience Letters.** Upon termination and completion of all exit formalities, the Company will provide you with such relieving, experience, or service certificates as may be required by applicable Pakistani labour law and the Company’s policies.

## 11. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution

11.1 **Governing Law.** This Offer Letter and your employment relationship with the Company shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, without regard to conflict‑of‑laws principles.

11.2 **Dispute Resolution.** Any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Offer Letter or your employment shall, in the first instance, be attempted to be resolved amicably between you and the Company. If not resolved amicably within a reasonable period, the dispute shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts and/or labour tribunals in **[BLANK – City/Province], Pakistan**, subject to any mandatory provisions of applicable labour law.

## 12. Entire Agreement and Amendments

12.1 **Entire Agreement.** This Offer Letter, together with any documents expressly referred to in it (including any confidentiality and IP assignment agreement and applicable Company policies), constitutes the entire understanding between you and the Company with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior discussions, representations, or offers, whether oral or written, relating to your employment with the Company.

12.2 **Amendments.** Any amendment or modification to this Offer Letter must be in writing and signed by an authorized representative of the Company. No oral statements or representations can amend the terms of this Offer Letter.

12.3 **Severability.** If any provision of this Offer Letter is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable under applicable law, such provision shall be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid and enforceable, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

## 13. Conditions Precedent

13.1 **Right to Work.** This Offer is conditional upon you having and maintaining, throughout your employment, the legal right to work in Pakistan. You agree to provide such documentation as the Company may reasonably require to verify your identity and right to work.

13.2 **Background Checks.** Where permitted by applicable law and Company policy, this Offer may be subject to satisfactory completion of background, reference, or other pre‑employment checks. Any such checks will be conducted in compliance with applicable law.

## 14. Acceptance of Offer

If you wish to accept this Offer, please sign and date the acknowledgment below and return a scanned copy to **[BLANK – Contact Email]** by **[BLANK – Offer Expiry Date]**, or such later date as the Company may agree in writing.

By signing below, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to the terms and conditions of this Offer Letter and that you are not subject to any contractual or other legal obligations that would prevent you from accepting or performing employment with the Company.

We look forward to welcoming you to Analytics Dojo SMC Private and to your contributions as a Data Analyst.

Yours sincerely,

For and on behalf of  
**Analytics Dojo SMC Private**

Name: **[BLANK – Authorized Signatory Name]**  
Title: **[BLANK – Authorized Signatory Title]**  
Date: **[BLANK]**

---

**Acknowledgment and Acceptance by Employee**

I, **Sara Khan**, hereby accept the offer of employment with Analytics Dojo SMC Private on the terms and conditions set out in this Offer Letter.

Signature: _______________________________  
Name: Sara Khan  
Date: _______________________________

---

> **Important – Attorney Review Disclaimer**  
> This draft Offer Letter has been prepared by an AI system based on limited instructions and general principles, and does **not** constitute legal advice. It may not reflect all requirements of Pakistani labour law or the specific policies and risk preferences of Analytics Dojo SMC Private. Before using, executing, or relying on this document, it **must** be reviewed, customized, and approved by a qualified lawyer licensed in the relevant jurisdiction(s). The Company and any users of this draft are solely responsible for obtaining appropriate legal advice and for all decisions made or actions taken in reliance on this document.
